evals.prompt: Review the JavaScript file for code style violations. List every issue you find.
evals.expected_output: Lists all violations: double quotes instead of single, missing semicolons, == instead of ===, != instead of !==, if blocks without curly braces, JSX props not alphabetically sorted (should be ariaLabel, className, role, userId).
assertions: Reports double quotes instead of single quotes
Reports == instead of === on the null check
Reports != instead of !== on the undefined check
Reports if statements missing curly braces
Reports JSX props not alphabetically sorted

evals.prompt: Write a TypeScript function that fetches a list of items from /api/items, filters for active ones, and returns them sorted by name. Follow all code style rules.
evals.expected_output: Uses single quotes, semicolons, ===, typed parameters and return type, curly braces on all control structures, 2-space indentation.
assertions: Uses single quotes for string literals
Semicolons present at end of statements
Function has typed parameters and return type
All if/for/while blocks have curly braces
